w:r.w:t: Prepared by: Moin Siddiqui, Founder & CEO
This document is strictly confidential. All metrics are sourced and referenced in Section 15.

HAY-M (Hay-Money) is a UK-based fintech mobile application designed to make saving and investing effortless, automated, and accessible to everyone — regardless of financial background or income level.

HAY-M connects to users' bank accounts via Open Banking APIs and automatically rounds up every card transaction to the nearest pound, sweeping the difference into a savings pot. Users set daily, monthly, and yearly saving goals, and choose how their accumulated savings grow — either via self-directed portfolios or an AI-powered investment advisor.
38% of UK adults have no regular savings habit
67% of 18–35s want automated saving tools but don't know where to start
Micro-investing is growing at 31% year-on-year, yet most platforms still require £500+ minimums
The UK household savings rate was just 6.7% in 2024 — well below the recommended 15–20%
UK adults ranked 15th out of 26 OECD nations in financial literacy
The UK fintech market grew from £11.2B in 2021 to an estimated £34.2B in 2026 — a 205% expansion
. Over 12 million UK adults are now on fintech apps
, and Open Banking infrastructure is mature enough to power real-time savings automation at scale.
Freemium subscription (£0 / £2.99 / £6.99 per month) + 0.35% AUM fee on invested assets + brand collaboration revenue.
Moin Siddiqui is investing £60,000 of personal capital to fund Year 1. A seed round of £300K–£750K will be targeted in Year 2 once a working MVP and early user traction metrics are established.
HAY-M is a mobile-first fintech application (iOS & Android) built around four principles: simplicity, automation, transparency, and personalisation.
HAY-M connects to users' current accounts via Open Banking (TrueLayer)
. Every card transaction is rounded up to the nearest £1 and the spare change swept automatically into the user's savings pot.
Users set saving targets at daily, monthly, and yearly levels. Smart nudges alert users falling behind, and the system auto-adjusts daily limits within user-defined boundaries.

A risk profiling questionnaire establishes each user's investment appetite. The AI advisor then constructs a diversified portfolio across UK equities, global ETFs, bonds, and alternative assets, with automated quarterly rebalancing.
HAY-M Plus and Pro subscribers choose from curated portfolios (Cautious, Balanced, Growth, Aggressive) or build their own from approved ETFs and funds.
An embedded AI assistant answers questions about investments, saving performance, and financial goals in plain English — within FCA-compliant guardrails.
Spending by category, saving rate over time, investment performance, and anonymised peer benchmarking. Weekly insight push notifications drive engagement and retention.
All data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256)
Biometric authentication (Face ID / fingerprint) as default app lock
Tokenisation of all bank credentials — HAY-M never stores raw account details
Annual third-party penetration testing from Day 1
ISO 27001 compliance roadmap initiated in Year 1
GDPR-compliant data architecture with full audit logging

The United Kingdom faces a profound and worsening savings and investment gap. Despite being one of the world's wealthiest economies, millions of UK adults are financially vulnerable — not through lack of income, but through lack of accessible, engaging, and automated tools.
Adults with no regular savings habit
Adults with no investment of any kind
38% of UK adults have no regular savings habit
, and the average savings rate of 6.7%
is less than half the recommended 15–20% for financial resilience. Cost-of-living pressures have made discretionary saving feel impossible for many young adults.
57% of UK adults hold no investments of any kind
. Most investment platforms require £500–£5,000 minimums, immediately excluding the majority of 18–30 year olds. Complex terminology further deters first-time investors.
Only 18% of UK adults use any form of micro-saving app, despite 67% expressing interest in automated tools
. Saving still relies on manual bank transfers — an act of willpower most people fail to sustain.
A 2023 OECD study ranked UK adults 15th out of 26 nations in financial literacy
. Young adults, immigrants, and gig economy workers are disproportionately affected.
HAY-M's competitive advantage rests on four strategic pillars that no single UK competitor currently combines in one product.
Round-up savings, basic goal setting, spending overview
Full analytics, multiple goals, curated portfolios
Full AI advisor, tax-loss harvesting, priority support
Sources: Competitor pricing and features verified against live product pages, April 2026

The UK micro-savings and investment app market has several established players. This section provides an honest, evidence-based assessment of each major competitor — their strengths, verified weaknesses, and how HAY-M is specifically designed to address the gaps they leave open.
Free tier + paid plans up to £9.99/month (pre-July 2025 plans) [22]
0.45% platform provider fee + fund management fee 0.06%–1.06% [24]
Regulated — FRN 836158 (Plum Fintech Ltd); FRN 739214 (Saveable Ltd) [28]
Strong automation: AI analyses spending and deposits automatically every few days
Broad product suite: Cash ISA, Lifetime ISA, Stocks & Shares ISA, SIPP, money market fund
2M+ users and strong brand recognition as 'Best UK Personal Finance App' (British Bank Awards 2023)
Invest from £1 with access to 3,000 stocks and 26 funds including Vanguard, BlackRock, Legal & General

Overly complex UX: One Google Play reviewer described it as 'design an app that makes saving as complex as possible' — to save into an ISA requires using a 'Brain', setting a rule, linking an account, depositing to a pot, then manually sending to the ISA monthly
Technical instability: App Store and Play Store reviews document frequent crashes, failed money transfers, and persistent 'something went wrong' errors
High fees for full access: Premium plans cost £9.99/month (pre-July 2025) — significantly above HAY-M Pro at £6.99
No personalised AI investment chat — automation is rules-based, not conversational
No daily/monthly/yearly goal hierarchy — goal setting is basic vs HAY-M's flexible D/M/Y structure

HAY-M is designed with radical simplicity at its core — a direct response to Plum's documented UX complexity. Where Plum requires 5+ steps to automate ISA contributions, HAY-M achieves the same outcome in 2 taps. HAY-M's AI chat advisor is also a genuine differentiator: rather than pre-set 'Brain' rules, users converse naturally with an AI that explains, adjusts, and coaches them — removing the need to understand product architecture to use the app.
Free savings; paid plans for full investing features [36]
Winner: Best Personal Finance App — British Bank Awards 2019, 2022, 2024, 2025 [33]
Consistently award-winning: Best Personal Finance App four times at British Bank Awards
Fast onboarding — reviewers note setup takes under 4 minutes
Prize Savings Account: monthly draw with prizes up to £75,000 — a novel savings incentive
Strong autosave AI — sets aside money users 'won't even miss' within 24 hours of setup
High app store ratings reflecting genuine user satisfaction
Limited investment choice: only 15 funds available — no individual stocks, no ISA stock-picking
Technical reliability issues: multiple documented 'connectivity issues' during use; reviewers note app becomes unreliable during high-activity periods
No phone support; weekday-only chat and email support — a significant gap for users needing help at weekends
No flexible goal hierarchy (daily/monthly/yearly targets) — saving goals are simpler and less customisable than HAY-M
Company described as having 'lost its way' by some long-term users who felt it shifted away from its core mission of helping low earners start saving
No AI investment advisory chat — automation is pre-set, not conversational

HAY-M directly addresses Chip's two biggest gaps: investment breadth and AI personalisation. While Chip locks investors into 15 funds, HAY-M offers self-directed portfolios with multi-asset allocation plus a full AI advisor that builds a personalised strategy. HAY-M also offers 7-day in-app support and a daily/monthly/yearly goal framework that gives users far more granular control over their saving behaviour.
Full current account, debit card, FSCS-protected deposits up to £85K
Largest UK challenger bank by customer base — 7.5M+ users and £3.7B valuation
Full current account with FSCS protection — users trust Monzo with primary banking
Strong savings pots and Flex credit product — broad financial ecosystem
Excellent brand recognition and community among young UK adults
No investment product — Monzo does not offer any equity, fund, or portfolio investing
Round-up savings are limited — no automatic investment of rounded-up funds
No AI investment advisor of any kind
Premium plan costs £15/month — significantly more expensive than HAY-M Pro at £6.99
Savings pots earn interest but offer no wealth-building investment pathway
Positioned as a bank, not a wealth platform — different user intent

Monzo and HAY-M serve complementary rather than directly competing needs — Monzo is a primary bank, HAY-M is a savings and investment companion. However, HAY-M directly captures users who have a Monzo current account but want to do more with their money than earn savings pot interest. HAY-M's round-ups can link to any UK current account (including Monzo), turning passive spending into active investing — a use case Monzo itself cannot fulfil.
No monthly fee; 0.75% AUM fee (fully managed) [13]
J.P. Morgan — one of the world's largest banks
Established robo-advisor with J.P. Morgan backing — institutional credibility
Genuine portfolio management with socially responsible investing (SRI) options
ISA and SIPP wrappers — tax-efficient long-term wealth building
Transparent fee structure with no hidden charges
£500 minimum investment — immediately excludes HAY-M's core 18–35 target audience who may have only £50–200 to start with
No savings automation or round-up features — purely an investment platform
No AI conversational advisor — portfolio is managed by algorithms, not explained by chat
Passive, not active — users deposit and wait; no engagement mechanics or goal tracking
No free tier — cost starts at 0.75% AUM from day one, making it expensive for small balances
Designed for savers with existing capital, not for building wealth from scratch

Nutmeg serves a fundamentally different customer — someone who already has £500+ and wants a 'set and forget' robo-advisor. HAY-M serves the customer who doesn't yet have that £500 and needs help building it. HAY-M's round-up mechanic, zero minimums, and AI coach make it the logical first step before a user might ever consider Nutmeg — positioning HAY-M not as a competitor but as the entry point for the next generation of UK investors.
The HAY-M Gap in the Market

No single competitor combines all five of HAY-M's defining capabilities in one app at one accessible price point: (1) round-up automation, (2) full AI investment advisor, (3) conversational AI coaching, (4) flexible D/M/Y goal hierarchy, and (5) self-directed portfolios. Plum comes closest but fails on UX simplicity and AI depth. Chip wins on user satisfaction but lacks investment breadth and weekend support. Monzo dominates banking but doesn't invest. Nutmeg invests well but excludes those without existing capital.

HAY-M is engineered specifically to occupy the gap left by all four — serving the underserved majority of UK adults who want to save automatically, invest intelligently, and understand what their money is doing, without needing to be financially literate to begin.
Demographics: Age 18–35, employed or self-employed, income £18K–£45K, urban UK
67% in this cohort express desire for automated saving tools
Most likely to be on fintech apps: 12M+ UK fintech users are predominantly 18–40
Demographics: Age 22–45, freelancers, delivery drivers, variable income
No employer pension; highly responsive to automatic, variable savings (round-ups scale with spending)
Gig economy comprises ~5M UK workers as of 2024
Age 18–25; financially independent for first time; high digital adoption
Goal: build savings habits from scratch; low risk tolerance; educational value of AI chat
All UK adults 18–55 with smartphone + bank account
UK adults 18–40 open to fintech micro-saving
3-year realistic capture (120K users by end of 2029)
Regulatory compliance is a foundational priority for HAY-M. The FCA maintains high standards for consumer finance products, and HAY-M will engage the regulatory pathway from Day 1.
Phase 1 (Q2–Q3 2026): Apply for FCA Regulatory Sandbox
— allows testing with real users under supervisory oversight before full authorisation
Phase 2 (Q3 2026 – Q2 2027): Operate as Appointed Representative (AR)
under an FCA-authorised principal firm while full application is processed
Phase 3 (Q3 2027): Achieve direct FCA authorisation with permissions for arranging deals in investments, communicating financial promotions, and operating as an e-money institution
Onfido automated identity verification at onboarding [18]
All marketing reviewed by compliance consultant pre-publication
Outcome-based product design; regular consumer outcome testing
AI advice framed as 'guidance' until full advisory permissions granted
Real-time anomaly detection; FCA-compliant audit trail
— automated ID verification, document checks, liveness detection
Custodian/Broker: DriveWealth or Alpaca — FCA-authorised; client assets held in segregated accounts
Legal Counsel: Specialist FCA regulatory solicitor engaged Q2 2026

0.35% annual fee on all assets under management. At 45,000 users in Year 3, with average AUM of £1,500 per investing user (40% of users investing), this generates approximately £94,500 annually — growing rapidly as user assets compound.

Referral commissions from complementary financial brands (insurance, cashback platforms, budgeting tools). Target: £55K in Year 3, scaling to £150K+ in Year 4. All partnerships curated to maintain user trust and FCA compliance.
Financial coaching sessions (£15/session), premium portfolio analytics add-on (£0.99/month), group savings challenges with brand-sponsored prizes.
Waitlist via LinkedIn thought leadership, TikTok personal finance content, Reddit (r/UKPersonalFinance)
2–3 UK micro-influencers in personal finance niche (10K–100K followers) for authentic product previews
'HAY-M Beta Access' email campaign — early sign-ups receive 3 months free HAY-M Plus
Target earned media in AltFi, Finextra, Sifted
Founder Moin Siddiqui builds personal brand as UK fintech entrepreneur
App Store and Google Play launch with curated review strategy (target 4.5+ rating)
Referral programme: £5 HAY-M credit for every friend who makes their first round-up
Google Ads pilot: 'save money app UK', 'micro investing UK', 'round up savings'
PR outreach: BBC Money Box, Guardian Money, ThisIsMoney — UK fintech founder story angle
University campus ambassador programme: 5 UK universities in Q1 2027
Scale paid digital acquisition across Meta, Google, TikTok — targeting £6–8 CAC
Employer savings partnerships: HR departments at mid-size UK companies
Open Banking data partnerships: cross-referrals with Emma, Snoop
30-day savings challenges with prize pools — viral sharing mechanic
Weekly 'Money Snapshot' push notification — personalised savings summary every Monday
In-app streak rewards: 30-day saving streak unlocks a HAY-M badge
Goal milestone celebrations: confetti + shareable card on reaching a savings target
Monthly 'HAY-M Digest' email — market insights, tips, user stories
HAY-M is cloud-native, microservices-oriented on AWS — designed to scale from hundreds to hundreds of thousands of concurrent users without architectural rework.
Single codebase for iOS and Android
Round-up calculation, pot management, goal tracking
Account linking, transaction feed, balance data

Risk profiling questionnaire (10 questions) feeds into a machine learning model. Output is an asset allocation across equities (UK, US, Emerging Markets), bonds, and alternatives. Rebalancing triggers when any asset class drifts more than 5% from target. Tax-loss harvesting logic flags opportunities quarterly for Pro subscribers.

ML model analyses spending patterns via Open Banking transaction feed to suggest achievable saving targets. Adapts daily round-up multipliers automatically when users set or change goals. Sends predictive nudges 48 hours before a user is likely to fall short of a weekly target.
Powered by OpenAI API with a custom system prompt constraining responses to FCA-compliant guidance (not regulated advice). All conversations logged for compliance audit trail.
Microservices in Docker containers via AWS ECS / Fargate
PostgreSQL with read replicas for high-throughput queries
Redis caching layer for frequently accessed user data
99.9% uptime SLA target; multi-AZ deployment from launch
Blue/green deployment pipeline for zero-downtime releases
AWS, third-party APIs, dev tooling, licenses
Vs £60K own capital — see funding gap strategy
Moin Siddiqui is a technology professional with a proven track record in quality assurance, test automation, and agile product delivery — built across international projects at
, one of the world's leading digital transformation and IT services firms (NYSE: WIT; 230,000+ employees across 65 countries).

At Wipro, Moin worked across on-/off-shore international project teams, demonstrating the ability to coordinate across cultures, time zones, and complex delivery environments — skills that directly transfer to building a distributed fintech team for HAY-M. Colleagues and senior leadership have described him as:
"A very skilful tester with great test automation know-how and experience. A great asset to each agile team."
"A reliable QA who asks the right questions... He keeps abreast with trending technologies and has the inclination to learning new skills. He would be an asset to any team for his sheer dedication."
Built automated test pipelines at enterprise scale (Wipro)
Quality-first engineering culture from Day 1 — critical for a regulated fintech handling real user funds
Worked in agile teams across international on/off-shore projects
Manages the 2-developer sprint cycle with discipline; knows how to scope MVPs and prevent scope creep
Proactively self-enrolled in automation classes without being asked
Entrepreneurial mindset — will master fintech regulation, AI product design, and investor relations independently
Strong foundation for investor communications, partner negotiations, and eventual team scaling post-seed
Received Super Achiever Award from CEO-level leadership at Wipro
Demonstrates delivery under pressure and recognition at the highest level — essential quality for a founder

Responsible for Node.js backend, PostgreSQL database, and API integrations (Open Banking, payment rails, custodian). Owns the savings engine, round-up logic, and transaction processing infrastructure.
Responsible for the React Native mobile application across iOS and Android. Owns the user interface, onboarding flow, dashboard, and notification system.
FCA Legal Advisor — regulatory licensing, financial promotions compliance, Consumer Duty
Investment Advisor — CFA-qualified; oversight of AI portfolio methodology and investment compliance
Growth Advisor — UK fintech growth marketer with app launch track record
Technology Advisor — senior engineer with AWS/financial services scaling experience
Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) — FCA authorisation and ongoing compliance management
Head of Growth — user acquisition, referral programme, and partnership channels
Customer Success Lead — onboarding, support, NPS improvement
Company formation · Hire 2 developers · FCA sandbox application [14] · UI/UX design · Tech stack locked
Core savings engine · Open Banking integration [17] · KYC/Onfido flow [18] · Internal alpha testing · Seed deck prepared · Waitlist 1,000+
App Store + Google Play · Revenue begins · Referral programme · 500 users Month 1 · FCA AR status active [15]
Seed round £300K–£750K · Team to 5+ FTE · CCO hired · AI advisor deployed · 10K MAU target · Full FCA authorisation [14]
Break-even Month 36 (base case) · 120K MAU by end 2029 · Series A discussions · EU expansion scoped
Moin Siddiqui invests £60,000 of personal capital. Total Year 1 budget is £118,000 — leaving a gap of £58,000 to be bridged through:
Pre-sales: beta access at £1.99/month to waitlist users pre-launch
Angel outreach: 3–5 UK fintech angels for a £20–30K bridge in Q3 2026
Innovate UK Smart Grant: application Q3 2026 (up to £50K available)
Pursued in H1 2027 once the following conditions are met:
Paid acquisition, partnerships, PR, referral programme
FCA full authorisation, CCO hire, ongoing legal

The following SWOT analysis provides a structured assessment of HAY-M's internal position and external environment as of April 2026, drawing on market data, competitor research, and the business plan assumptions outlined in preceding sections.
Zero investment minimum — round-ups from as little as £0.01 enable true micro-investing
Only UK app combining full AI advisory + self-directed portfolios + micro-saving + conversational AI coach in one product
Accessible pricing (£0–£6.99/mo) vs competitors charging up to £15/mo or 0.75% AUM
Lean Year 1 cost base — 2 developers capped at £60K total; founder-led with low overhead
Radically simple UX — designed to eliminate the complexity that Plum and Chip users complain about
Founder brings QA, agile delivery, and cross-team collaboration skills from Wipro (global IT enterprise)
Multi-stream revenue model (subscriptions + AUM + brand collaborations) reduces single-source dependency
No existing user base, brand recognition, or track record — starting from zero in a crowded market
Year 1 funding gap of £58K — own capital (£60K) does not fully cover projected budget (£118K)
Small engineering team (2 developers) limits feature velocity — risk of slower MVP delivery vs well-funded rivals
FCA regulatory process is slow and expensive — authorisation delays can stall product launch and investor confidence
No existing compliance infrastructure — FCA Appointed Representative model limits product scope in Year 1
AI chat quality depends on OpenAI API — third-party dependency introduces cost, latency, and quality risks
Founder has no prior fintech or startup founding experience — learning curve in investor relations and regulatory navigation
UK fintech market growing from £11.2B (2021) to estimated £34.2B (2026) — a 205% expansion creating space for new entrants
38% of UK adults have no savings habit — massive underserved TAM of financially disengaged consumers
Mature Open Banking infrastructure (TrueLayer, Plaid) removes technical barriers that previously blocked new entrants
Competitor UX frustration is documented and widespread — Plum and Chip users actively seek simpler alternatives
Micro-investing growing 31% YoY — tailwinds already in market; HAY-M arrives at the right time
AI cost reduction — OpenAI API pricing trends downward, improving HAY-M's unit economics over time
Innovate UK Smart Grants (up to £50K) and UK angel networks actively funding early-stage fintech in 2026
Employer financial wellbeing partnerships — growing HR trend creates B2B acquisition channel beyond direct-to-consumer
European expansion via FCA passporting from 2029 — Republic of Ireland as first market (English-speaking, similar regulation)
Monzo, Plum, or Chip could clone HAY-M's AI chat and goal features — large incumbents have resources to respond quickly to competitive threats
FCA regulatory tightening — evolving Consumer Duty rules and AI-in-finance guidance could increase compliance costs unexpectedly
User trust barriers — financial data sharing anxiety among UK consumers may slow adoption, especially in post-breach news cycles
UK economic downturn or cost-of-living worsening — reduces discretionary spending and therefore round-up volumes
Seed funding market tightening — UK VC investment in fintech declined post-2022; raising £300K–£750K may be harder than projected
Key developer attrition — loss of either Year 1 engineer before MVP launch would critically delay product delivery
OpenAI API disruption — pricing changes, API deprecation, or quality regression could impact the AI advisor feature mid-development
Market saturation — with 12M+ UK users already on fintech apps, differentiation must be sharp and consistent to attract switchers
SO — Use Strengths to capture Opportunities
Launch with radical simplicity + AI chat as core differentiator; target Plum/Chip frustrated users directly in marketing
Use market timing to bridge gaps
Apply for Innovate UK grant early; leverage FCA sandbox timeline to build credibility with investors before full launch
ST — Use Strengths to counter Threats
Build community moat before incumbents react
Focus on user habit formation and referral loop — community stickiness makes incumbent cloning less effective
Scope MVP tightly; maintain contingency reserve; get FCA compliance right first time to avoid regulatory setbacks
Engage FCA sandbox Q2 2026 [14]; compliance consultant Day 1
Agile 2-week sprints; feature-scoped MVP; monthly KPIs
Pre-launch waitlist; influencer beta; referral incentives
Bootstrap lean; 5+ investors approached in parallel Q3 2026
Differentiate on AI quality; build community lock-in first
Annual pen testing; ISO 27001 roadmap; cyber insurance Day 1
Equity package; thorough documentation; knowledge sharing
Counter-cyclical positioning: 'save smarter during hard times'
All metrics, market data, competitor comparisons, and regulatory references cited in this business plan are sourced from the publications listed below. Sources were verified in April 2026.
FCA Financial Lives Survey 2024 — Savings & Debt chapter
KPMG Pulse of Fintech H2 2024 — UK Market
Statista — Number of Fintech App Users UK 2025
OECD/INFE 2023 International Survey of Adult Financial Literacy
Deloitte Centre for Financial Services — UK Digital Banking Report 2024
Similarweb / App Annie — UK Personal Finance App Rankings Q1 2025
Bank of England — Fintech Regulatory Landscape 2024
Moin Siddiqui — LinkedIn Profile (linkedin.com/in/moinas), peer recommendations, April 2026
Wise / Money To The Masses — Plum Review 2026
Up The Gains — Plum App Review 2026
Plum — Google Play Store reviews, April 2026
Chip — Smart Money People community reviews
Finder UK — Chip App Review 2025 (customer satisfaction survey)
Nuts About Money — Chip savings app review

Competitor pricing data ([10]–[13]) was retrieved directly from live product pricing pages in April 2026 and is subject to change. Market size estimates ([4][5][6]) are derived from analyst reports and may vary. Financial projections in Section 10 are internal estimates based on disclosed sources and standard UK fintech industry benchmarks. They do not constitute a guarantee of future performance.
Registered in England & Wales | Confidential — April 2026
